allowed_files: READ-ONLY — no writes to any production file
purpose: Map the full current risk pipeline end-to-end. Produce a written audit document that is the factual foundation all later phases build on. Nothing is changed in this phase. The audit document is the deliverable.

in_scope: Read core/risk.py — all entry gates, exit triggers, held-side price logic, SL cluster detection, thresholds
Read core/paper_exec.py — sizing logic, open_position, close_position, held-contract semantics, fee simulation
Read bot_core.py — exit loop, entry loop, concurrency caps, _market_cooldown, bridge integration, _dummy_market path
Read dashboard_server.py — bankroll display, session accounting, available cash, capital committed, live equity
Read core/types.py — canonical Trade, Market, OBSnapshot, Signal dataclasses
Read .env — all risk threshold env vars currently in use
Read core/db.py — how trades are persisted, what fields exist, how pnl_usd/exit_px/reason_close are stored
out_of_scope: Do not audit the MLB model strategy or recommendation_api.py unless a field it emits directly affects risk sizing
Do not audit dashboard UI aesthetics

entry_pipeline: What gates exist before a trade opens? List all in order.
Where is position sizing calculated? What inputs does it use?
What concurrency cap is enforced and where?
What is the _market_cooldown mechanism and where is it checked vs set?
What does _dummy_market() produce and what risk does it carry?
held_side_pricing: What is _held_bid()? Where is it defined? What does it return for BUY_YES vs BUY_NO?
Where is current_held_price derived at runtime?
Is there any path where raw YES/NO bid is used instead of held-side bid? Flag each one.
What happens if _held_bid() returns None? Trace every callsite.
tp_sl_exits: List every exit reason in check_exit() with its trigger condition and the exact math.
What are the current env var thresholds for each? (AUTO_TAKE_PROFIT_PCT, AUTO_STOP_LOSS_PCT, NEAR_RESOLUTION_PRICE, TRAILING_STOP_ACTIVATE_PCT, TRAILING_STOP_DRAWDOWN_PCT, TIME_EXIT_BUFFER_SECONDS)
What cooldowns are set after each exit type? (near_resolution: 600s, stop_loss: 1800s, gap_stop: 3600s — verify these are live)
Is there any exit path that does not log a CLOSE entry? Flag it.
Is there any exit path that does not persist exit_px and reason_close to DB? Flag it.
sizing: What is the exact sizing formula in paper_exec.py?
What inputs feed it: confidence, bankroll, max_size_usd, min_size_usd, concurrency?
Are there confidence tiers? List them.
Is size capped per-trade and per-session? Where?
What is committed_usd at any given moment — how is it computed?
bankroll_accounting: What is the canonical definition of: lifetime_bankroll, session_baseline, session_pnl, available_cash, capital_committed?
Where is each computed (dashboard_server.py, DB query, runtime state)?
Is there any path where closed trade PnL is double-counted?
Is there any fake reset mechanism that erases lifetime history? Flag it.
What does the DB return for total realized PnL? Is it cumulative since inception?
concurrency_and_caps: What is the MAX_CONCURRENT_TRADES cap and where is it enforced?
Is there a per-market cap (max 1 open position per market_id)?
Is there a per-session max loss cap? If not, note the gap.
Is there a daily max loss cap? If not, note the gap.

result_must_include: broken_invariants: list of places held-side truth is violated or 'none'
immediate_risks: list of high-severity findings requiring immediate attention
report_must_include: Entry pipeline map — ordered list of gates
TP/SL exit map — every trigger with threshold values
Held-side pricing map — every callsite of _held_bid()
Bankroll/session accounting map — every variable and where computed
Gaps table: surface | gap description | severity (HIGH/MED/LOW)
Broken invariants table: location | what invariant | what actually happens
Recommended phase priorities based on findings

preflight_questions: Is core/risk.py readable and importable without side effects?
Does bot_core.py compile cleanly (python -m py_compile bot_core.py)?
Are all imports in core/types.py resolvable?
acceptance_criteria: Report covers all 6 audit surfaces listed above
Every gap and broken invariant is listed with file:line_number reference
No production file was modified (git diff --name-only shows nothing)
Immediate risks are explicitly flagged and ranked
proof_of_work: Include file:line_number citations for every claim in the report
For every threshold, show the exact env var name and current value from .env
For every sizing formula, show the exact code lines
Do not paraphrase code — quote relevant lines directly
guardrails: Do not redesign strategy or model logic
Do not reintroduce ambiguous YES/NO economics
Held-side contract truth must remain the basis for pricing and exit logic
Distinguish clearly: VERIFIED | PARTIAL | BLOCKED | SUPERSEDED
